narrative.headline: 2019 Honda Accord: engine and auto-braking lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Engine and forward collision avoidance issues together account for the largest share of owner complaints, with engine problems including reported fires and crashes making them the most safety-prominent concern. Owners across multiple component areas describe vehicles stalling, losing power, or behaving unexpectedly while in motion on highways. Electrical system and fuel/propulsion faults also appear frequently, sometimes with crash involvement, pointing to a broad pattern of unexpected loss of vehicle control.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine losing power or cutting out during highway driving, including incidents where the vehicle was accelerating to pass at speed on wet roads. · 1 fire · 3 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report warning indicators illuminating unexpectedly, with some incidents involving the tire pressure monitoring system activating alongside other concerns. · 8 crash · 9 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle stopping without warning in the middle of highway travel, sometimes creating a rear-collision hazard from following traffic. · 1 fire · 4 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle beginning to bog and lose throttle response on the highway at very low mileage, with a VSA modulator identified by dealerships as the failed component. · 4 crash · 6 injured

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 86 complaints (14%), with a notable 7 crashes and 7 injuries. A braking issue worth checking carefully during a test drive or inspection.
